questions.question: A student performs three actions with a magnet and a solenoid connected to a galvanometer. Which row in the table correctly predicts the observations?nnAction | Observationn--- | ---n1. Magnet moving in | Deflectionn2. Magnet stationary inside | No deflectionn3. Magnet moving out | Opposite deflection
questions.explanation:
These observations are standard for electromagnetic induction. Deflection only occurs during relative motion, and reversing the motion reverses the deflection.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A magnet is moved towards a coil of wire. Which of the following does NOT increase the size of the induced e.m.f.?
options: Using a thicker wire with lower resistance
Increasing the number of turns in the coil
questions.explanation:
While thicker wire may increase the induced current ($I$), it does not affect the magnitude of the induced e.m.f. ($V$), which depends on flux and turns.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: The diagram shows the North pole of a magnet moving towards a solenoid.n[N-S Magnet] $rightarrow$ [Solenoid End A]nWhat is the polarity induced at End A and what is the nature of the force between them?
questions.explanation:
To oppose the North pole coming towards it, End A must become a North pole. Like poles (N and N) repel each other.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: The graph shows the induced e.m.f. in a coil over time. Which feature of the graph indicates that the magnet was moving faster as it left the coil than when it entered?n[Graph shows a small first peak and a tall second peak in the opposite direction]
options: The height of the second peak is greater than the first
The second peak is in the opposite direction to the first
The area under the two peaks is the same
The time duration for both peaks is identical
questions.explanation:
Induced e.m.f. magnitude is proportional to speed. A taller peak on the graph indicates a higher induced e.m.f., which implies the magnet was moving faster at that time.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A wire is moved upwards through a horizontal magnetic field that points from North to South. In which direction is the current induced in the wire? (Hint: Use Fleming's right-hand rule)
questions.explanation:
Using Fleming's Right-Hand Rule: Thumb (Motion) up, First finger (Field) N to S (right), Second finger (Current) points out of the page.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
